---
title: "UIAO Governance Findings"
subtitle: "Constraints identified in the substrate's environment"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
description: "First-class operational artifacts documenting constraints outside UIAO's direct control that materially affect its capability. Authorized by ADR-030 §5.2."
categories: [findings, governance, operations]
---

Governance findings are the first-class artifact class UIAO uses
to document **constraints outside its direct control that
materially affect its capability**. They are authorized by
[ADR-030](../../core/canon/adr/adr-030-pre-uiao-terminology-reconciliation.md)
§5.2.

A finding is not canon (canon documents what the substrate IS).
A finding is not narrative (narrative explains; findings record
facts with an owner). A finding is a reader-facing operational
record of a constraint, its evidence, its capability gap, and its
proposed remedy.

## Why findings have a dedicated class

The alternative would be to let constraints live as paragraphs in
narrative pages or as issues in someone's ticket queue. Both lose
their ownership trail the moment the author moves on. A finding
with its own frontmatter, status, and lifecycle is auditable —
future operators can see what was known, when, and what was done
about it. That is a governance artifact, not a complaint.

The organizational principle cited in ADR-030 comes from the UIAO
program's current CIO: **"everyone owns all problems they
identify."** That principle alone justifies the class. A finding
documents identification; the class structure documents ownership;
the status lifecycle documents resolution.

## Status values

| Status | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `Open` | Finding identified; remediation path not yet chosen |
| `Awaiting-External-Remediation` | Remedy requires action by a party outside the substrate's authority |
| `Remediated-Internally` | UIAO implemented a workaround; finding retired with supersession pointer to canon |
| `Retired-Superseded` | External remediation landed; finding retired; evidence preserved |

## Active findings

::: {.callout-note}
No findings are currently published. The first finding
(`fedramp-gcc-moderate-telemetry-constraint.md` — M365 Informed
Network Routing unavailability in GCC-Moderate) is queued for
publication once its citations are finalized. Expected status on
publication: `Awaiting-External-Remediation` paired with an
internal-remediation proposal in the same document.
:::

## Class contract

Every finding documents:

1. **Constraint** — what the substrate has identified.
2. **Evidence** — citations to authoritative sources, dated.
3. **Capability gap** — what the substrate cannot do because of
   the constraint.
4. **Proposed remedy** — split into **internal** (UIAO-only)
   and **external** (another party).
5. **Ownership trail** — who identified, what escalations
   happened, who currently owns it.
6. **Status and lifecycle** — per the table above.

The full class contract is in
[`docs/findings/README.md`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/docs/findings/README.md).
